ksa_text: Ability to develop comprehensive privacy management frameworks, policies, and compliance procedures that address organizational privacy obligations and regulatory requirements.

dcwf.top_element_label: Communicate the results of AI risk assessments to relevant stakeholders.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Communicate the results of AI risk assessments to relevant stakeholders.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Security & Platform Strategy is a primary or secondary domain for the Director of Data Governance. This ability KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (director).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Security & Platform Strategy), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.5). External reviewers may question whether this specific ability KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the Director of Data Governance versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: NIST CSF, CMU SEI enterprise security frameworks, and CDAIO job market data consistently include security and platform strategy oversight for senior data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Security & Platform Strategy appearing in job postings and role definition literature for Director of Data Governance.

decision_rationale: Security & Platform Strategy ability KSA is within scope for the Director of Data Governance at the director tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
